_meta.title: @agent-ui/code M1 — code+prose family: core seams + ./highlight + ./markdown (build decomposition)
_meta.author: system-planner
_meta.grounding: ADR-0119 (accepted 2026-07-10, all forks answered — keep @agent-ui/code; ts/js·json·html·css·python·shell·md-fences; markdown subset WITH GFM tables; hunks-diff at M2; ui-markdown in ./markdown) + prd/code-prose-family.prd.md (accepted v1.0). Realizes ADR-0119 cl.1-4,6-8 (cl.5 ui-diff is M2). Upstream precedent (cited, not copied): @agent-ui/icons (registry + subpath + self-registration — ADR-0065/0066: registry.ts signal-free, index.ts core-only barrel, phosphor/index.ts self-registers) · @agent-ui/router (sibling-package skeleton, layering.test.ts raw-glob scan, barrels.test.ts tree-shake crawl, measure-size.mjs marginal row — ADR-0115) · controls/code (the UNCHANGED ui-code host — ADR-0113 host-as-content, escape hatch (a), plain-wins clobber; this wave touches NO file under packages/agent-ui/components) · controls/text (as='a'+href fail-closed scheme gate href.ts, SAFE_HREF_SCHEMES — ADR-0114; markdown links inherit it) · controls/table (columns/rows JSON model, resolveCell hardening — GFM tables) · descriptor/component-descriptor.ts (validateComponentDescriptor + compareDescriptorToProps, exported at @agent-ui/components/descriptor — the per-package trip-wire router uses). Verified 2026-07-10: ui-text `as` enum = [none,h1..h6,p,span,blockquote,a]; the components SPEC-N2 gates (site-coverage/canon/toc) scan ONLY components/src/{controls,components} — so ui-markdown (in code/src/markdown/) owes NO components allowlist entry (ADR-0119 cl.7 package-bound-gate interpretation, confirmed).
_meta.note: Both planes derived independently. OUTSIDE-IN from the package's structural grain (skeleton -> core seams -> highlight pack -> markdown pack -> gates -> barrel/records). INSIDE-OUT from the ask's verb set (stand-in-DAG, fence-layering, type-the-token-stream, hold-the-active-engine, tokenize, project-to-light-DOM, keep-byte-identical, tokenize-seven-langs, self-register, map-token-roles, parse-the-subset, render-to-fleet-DOM, forward-language+compose, sanitize-by-absence, hold-the-source-prop, descriptor+trip-wire, prove-identity, prove-tree-shake, prove-structure-cross-engine, hold-budgets+review, keep-catalog-untouched, export-surface, repair-CLAUDE.md) plus standing fleet doctrine (layering, per-pack budget, cross-engine truth, descriptors, independent review, context-is-memory). Design records: ADR-0119 (accepted) · prd/code-prose-family.prd.md (accepted) · spec/code-prose.spec.md (SPEC-C1..C11) · lld/code-prose.lld.md (LLD-C1..C11). THREE SPEC-level interpretations flagged in the SPEC (I-1 native <em>/<strong>/<code> for inline; I-2 native <ul>/<ol>/<li> for lists — the fleet ui-list EXISTS but is a role=list flex stack that renders no <li>/ordinal/nesting, dismissed on mechanics; I-3 GFM table cells plain-text at v1) — element-vocabulary mechanism choices the ADR left to SPEC/LLD, not contract forks. M2 (ui-diff + consumer catalog-extension recipe) is sketch-only, not in this manifest.

nodes.label: @agent-ui/code M1
package infrastructure
package skeleton (package.json exports map: core `.` + ./highlight + ./markdown + their .css; tsconfig extending the strict base; root workspace row)
nodes.accept: `npm run check` exits 0 with @agent-ui/code resolving; dependencies exactly {@agent-ui/components, @agent-ui/shared}; the `.` export points at src/index.ts and each pack at its own subpath; no other package.json changed (LLD-C1)

nodes.label: layering trip-wire (code/src/layering.test.ts — inward-only import scan + apex-un-imported inward walk)
nodes.accept: `npm test` includes code layering.test.ts green: every import under code/src resolves to {@agent-ui/components, @agent-ui/shared} or a local path; no source under components/src, a2ui/src, shared/src imports @agent-ui/code (structural catalog fence); a planted upward import on a unique token goes RED (negative control, grep-confirmed applied then reverted) — SPEC-C1 AC1/AC2, LLD-C2

nodes.label: core seams (code/src/core/ — zero-dep, signal-free)
token stream type (core/token.ts — Token{kind,text}, TokenKind 6-tier union, Highlighter fn type, the roundTrips predicate)
nodes.accept: token.test.ts green: TokenKind is the six literals; roundTrips(tokens,code) is true iff concatenated text === code and BITES on a gap/overlap fixture (SPEC-C2, LLD-C3)

nodes.label: highlighter registry (core/registry.ts — Registry class + highlighterRegistry singleton + registerHighlighter/tokenize; last-wins; verbatim-when-empty; boundary round-trip enforcement) + no-kernel gate
nodes.accept: registry + no-kernel suites green: empty registry -> tokenize returns [{plain,code}]; a second registerHighlighter replaces the first (last-wins); two Registry instances are isolated; a misbehaving highlighter (non-round-tripping) is downgraded to a single plain token at the boundary; a planted components-kernel/DOM-signal import in core/*.ts goes RED (SPEC-C2 AC1/AC2/AC3, LLD-C4)

nodes.label: projection seam (core/project.ts — projectHighlight(host,code,language): tokens -> light-DOM <span data-token> children; empty path = single text node; textContent never innerHTML)
nodes.accept: project.test.ts green: with a registered highlighter the host children are [data-token] spans whose concatenated text === code exactly (a keyword span for `const`); with an EMPTY registry the host holds ONE text node === code and zero spans (byte-identical, the identity level); a subsequent plain textContent write clobbers the spans (plain-wins) — SPEC-C3 AC1/AC2/AC3, LLD-C5

nodes.label: ./highlight pack (code/src/highlight/)
the seven tokenizers (highlight/langs/{ts,json,html,css,python,shell,markdown}.ts + a shared scan.ts lexer core — line-oriented, coarse 5-tier, fidelity-fenced)
nodes.accept: seven tokenizer suites green: each classifies its agent-real fixture into the expected coarse tier on the marked span AND round-trips exactly (unconditionally); unterminated string/comment and empty input never throw and round-trip; one planted misclassification fails per language (negative control) — SPEC-C4, LLD-C6

nodes.label: highlight self-registration + token CSS (highlight/index.ts — bundled dispatch-by-language highlighter, self-registers on import, registerHighlight() explicit; highlight.css — --ui-code-token-* role block + [data-token] consumption + forced-colors degrade)
nodes.accept: highlight suites green: `import '@agent-ui/code/highlight'` makes activeHighlighter() non-null and tokenize('// hi\nconst x=1','ts') yields comment/keyword/number tiers round-tripping; an unknown language -> [{plain,code}] no throw; highlight.css resolves each of the five --ui-code-token-* to a real --md-sys-color-* ON-SURFACE role (role-family only, NOT a numbered step — the sheet is mid-migration) and every [data-token] computes CanvasText under forced-colors (SPEC-C4/C5, LLD-C7)

nodes.label: ./markdown pack (code/src/markdown/) — the flagship
markdown parser (markdown/parse.ts — block+inline subset -> AST of KNOWN node kinds only; HTML-as-inert-text by absence of a raw-HTML lane) + injection corpus
nodes.accept: parse.test.ts + injection.test.ts green: the subset (headings, paragraphs, nested lists, blockquote, fenced code, inline em/strong/code, [text](url) links, GFM tables) parses to the AST; unterminated fences / ragged tables / unbalanced emphasis / raw <script> lines never throw and produce only text-or-known nodes (no HTML node kind exists); the injection corpus yields only `text` nodes and denied-scheme `link` nodes — SPEC-C6/C7, LLD-C8

nodes.label: markdown renderer + ui-markdown element (markdown/render.ts construct->fleet-DOM map, markdown.ts UIMarkdownElement + bindable `markdown` prop, markdown.css, markdown.md descriptor)
nodes.accept: markdown.test.ts + descriptor trip-wire green (jsdom): each construct renders its mapped fleet element (heading->ui-text as=hN, paragraph->ui-text as=p, list->native ul/ol/li nested, blockquote->ui-text as=blockquote, fenced->ui-code language= + projectHighlight, inline em/strong/code->native <em>/<strong>/<code>, link->ui-text as=a href= with ui-text's gate denying javascript:, GFM table->ui-table columns/rows plain-string cells); markdown='' clears children with no residue; renderer calls NO innerHTML and does NOT import ./highlight (compose via the registry only); validateComponentDescriptor([])==[] and compareDescriptorToProps(parsed.attributes, UIMarkdownElement.props)==[] (imported from @agent-ui/components/descriptor) — SPEC-C6/C7/C8/C9, LLD-C9

nodes.label: gates & proof
identity + tree-shake gates (identity.test.ts, barrels.test.ts)
nodes.accept: gate suites green: importing the `.` barrel registers NO custom element and NO highlighter and constructs no module-scope MutationObserver; projectHighlight with an empty registry is a single text node === code; the barrel source re-exports no ./highlight/./markdown module (grep); `./markdown` imported alone registers ui-markdown but leaves activeHighlighter() null (no tokenizer bytes); a structural note asserts the wave touched NO file under packages/agent-ui/components (the identity root) — SPEC-C1 AC3, SPEC-C3 AC2, SPEC-C8 AC1, SPEC-C9, LLD-C10

nodes.label: injection corpus + cross-engine browser legs (markdown.browser.test.ts — Chromium + WebKit)
nodes.accept: test:browser green in BOTH engines: rendered structure for each construct is non-zero and correctly nested; the injection corpus renders inert (zero <script> elements, no rendered on* attribute, raw tags visible as text, javascript: link denied); the forced-colors token-degrade leg holds when ./highlight is adopted (Chromium emulates, WebKit structural probe — instrument-bridge); each leg has a biting negative control — SPEC-C6/C7/C5, LLD-C10

nodes.label: per-pack size line-items + independent reviewer + catalog-disposition verification
nodes.accept: manual `npm run size` reports THREE @agent-ui/code rows (core `.`, ./highlight, ./markdown) each within its budget MEASURED-then-PINNED at kickoff (ADR-0080 — not guessed), the core row proving no pack mass; component-reviewer records GO (both axes >=4, zero blockers) on ui-markdown BEFORE the M1 commit; catalog/default/* and the components allowlist are byte-unchanged and every components SPEC-N2 gate stays green (default catalog untouched, no allowlist entry owed — the package-bound-gate interpretation, grep-confirmed) — SPEC-C9/C10, LLD-C10

nodes.label: core barrel (code/src/index.ts core-only — the full package.json exports map was landed at n1a; this slice writes ONLY the barrel body; serial integration slice, single writer)
nodes.accept: index.ts exports the token types + registry + projectHighlight ONLY — NEVER a pack module (grep, the router barrel precedent); each pack self-defines/self-registers on its own subpath import; `npm run check && npm test` green — SPEC-C1, SPEC-C10, LLD-C11a

nodes.label: CLAUDE.md DAG rows repaired in-change (Layout @agent-ui/code row + Conventions DAG line: code as a components sibling)
nodes.accept: CLAUDE.md's Layout list carries a @agent-ui/code row and the Conventions DAG line names `code` a sibling branch off components (never imported by a2ui/app at v1, like router); doc-review confirms no dangling reference (context-is-memory, same change) — SPEC-C11, LLD-C11b

actions.label: stand the package in the workspace DAG (resolvable under strict TS, correct dependency set)
enforce the layering law (imports inward-only; apex un-imported; structural catalog fence)
define the engine-agnostic token stream type (contiguous, round-trip-exact)
hold the active highlighter (signal-free registry, last-wins, instance + singleton)
tokenize by language — verbatim when none registered; enforce the round-trip at the boundary
project a token stream into a ui-code host as light-DOM spans (the escape-hatch seam)
keep the empty path byte-identical (a single text node — the identity level)
tokenize each v1 language coarsely (seven hand-rolled, line-oriented grammars, fidelity-fenced)
self-register the bundled highlighter on import (dispatch by language; unknown -> plain)
map token tiers to theme-honest --ui-code-token-* color roles + forced-colors degrade
parse the markdown subset (blocks + inline) with HTML-as-inert-text by construction
render the AST into real fleet DOM (the construct -> fleet-element map, no innerHTML)
forward a fence language to ui-code and compose with highlight through the registry (no import edge)
sanitize by construction — no raw-HTML lane exists, injection input renders inert
hold the markdown source as a bindable prop (the ui-markdown element)
carry a truthful descriptor + the per-package contract<->props trip-wire
prove the identity gate (core import registers nothing; empty path byte-identical; components untouched)
prove the tree-shake contract (core + ./markdown drag no tokenizer bytes)
prove rendered structure + injection inertness cross-engine (Chromium + WebKit)
hold per-pack size budgets (measured baseline) and pass independent review before commit
keep the default catalog untouched (no components allowlist entry owed; package-bound gate)
export the public surface (core barrel core-only + pack subpaths + css)
repair CLAUDE.md DAG rows in the same change (context-is-memory)

edges.why: the trip-wire scans a package that must exist and resolve first
the core token module lives in the package the skeleton creates
the markdown parser lives in the package the skeleton creates (independent of the core types)
the registry stores and returns the token type
the tokenizers emit the token type
the seam reads the registry's tokenize (single resolution point)
self-registration installs the bundled highlighter into the registry
the bundled highlighter dispatches to the seven tokenizers
the renderer projects fenced code through the seam
the renderer consumes the parser's AST
the ./highlight subpath finalizes after self-registration exists
the subpath exports finalize after the element and renderer exist
identity + tree-shake measure the finalized public surface, not a moving target
size measures the finalized surface, not a moving target
the browser legs render the element's output and exercise the injection corpus
the compose + forced-colors token leg needs the highlight pack registered
the reviewer/DoD gate covers the identity + tree-shake posture
the reviewer scores browser-proven behavior, not intentions
CLAUDE.md's DAG line records the finalized package shape
